task.name: Blind 8-dimension plan audit
task.objective: Read the on-disk plan artifacts (goals.yaml, goal.md files, docs/architecture/*), evaluate each goal against an 8-dimension checklist with a severity-weighted scoring rubric, and produce a verdict: approved (score >=90 with zero open SEV-1/SEV-2 findings) or a findings report requiring remediation.

glossary.term: Name of this auditor's tmux window.
Project root directory (cwd). All paths are relative to this.
Path to .tmux-cli/goals.yaml — the plan's goal definitions.
docs/architecture/plan-approval.md — written ONLY when score >=90 AND zero open SEV-1/SEV-2.
docs/architecture/plan-audit-N.md — written on fail with per-finding detail at file:line.
Current audit pass number (1-based). Incremented by the caller on each re-invocation.

scoring-rubric.note: Start at 100 points. Deduct per finding by severity. A plan passes when score >=90 AND zero open SEV-1 or SEV-2 findings remain.
scoring-rubric.severity: Critical: blocks execution outright or produces wrong results (missing validate, circular dependency, impossible acceptance criterion).
Major: likely to cause retry loops, cascading failures, or silent data corruption (ambiguous validate, tautological acceptance, missing depends_on edge).
Moderate: unclear spec that may confuse the executor or waste retries (vague scope, overlapping deliverables, under-specified investigation config).
Minor: style or convention violation that does not block execution (description over 120 chars, missing fan-out hints, non-standard phase name).

llm.mandate: NEVER modify any project files, goals, specs, or code — the auditor is strictly read-only
NEVER weaken validate commands or acceptance criteria to improve the score
NEVER invent scoring dimensions beyond the 8 defined in the checklist
NEVER use AskUserQuestion or any interactive prompt — all decisions are autonomous
NEVER edit goal.md, goals.yaml, or any code file — role separation is absolute
ONLY write docs/architecture/plan-approval.md (on pass) or docs/architecture/plan-audit-N.md (on fail)
One pass per invocation — the 3-pass stop condition is driven by the CALLER re-invoking plan-audit
reach 90% by fixing the plan, never by weakening validate/acceptance to make red turn green

step.title: MCP precondition and project context
step.action: Call windows-list to verify tmux-cli MCP is available.

check.action: Print exactly: ERROR: tmux-cli MCP required but not available — enable it and retry.

step.action: Check if AGENTS.md exists in the project root. If it does, Read it and internalize project conventions, architecture, and constraints.
If AGENTS.md does not exist, check for CLAUDE.md instead. Read whichever is found first.
If neither exists, proceed without project context.

step.title: Read on-disk artifacts (blind audit)
step.action: Read .tmux-cli/goals.yaml. Parse all goal entries: ID, description, status, phase, depends_on, validate, acceptance, scope, investigation_config.

check.action: Print: "ERROR: No goals.yaml found — nothing to audit."

step.action: For each goal, read .tmux-cli/goals/<goal-id>/goal.md to get the full goal specification.
Read all files under docs/architecture/ for cross-referencing (ADRs, bounded-contexts, api-endpoints, test-environment, cross-cutting, etc.).
Read any prior audit files: docs/architecture/plan-audit-*.md. Extract PASS_N as max(prior pass numbers) + 1, or 1 if no prior audits exist.
step.rule: This is a BLIND audit — never read planner conversation history, only on-disk artifacts.

step.title: Daemon's-eye validate evaluation
step.action: For each goal, evaluate its validate commands exactly as the daemon would run them:

substep.title: Command validity
substep.action: Check that each validate command is a concrete, runnable shell command (not prose, not a placeholder).
Verify commands reference real paths and tools available in the project stack.

substep.title: Dependency order
substep.action: Verify that depends_on edges form a valid DAG — no cycles, no missing references.
Verify phase ordering: gate before scaffold before domain before application before infrastructure before fixtures before actions/auth/events.

substep.title: CMD-CONV wrapping
substep.action: Verify each validate command is a bare command (the daemon wraps it). No shell wrappers, no && chains within a single validate entry.

step.title: 8-dimension checklist
step.action: Evaluate every goal against all 8 dimensions. Record each finding with: severity (SEV-1 through SEV-4), dimension name, goal ID, file:line reference, and a one-line description.

dimension.name: validate-executability
dimension.description: Every validate command must be a concrete, project-runnable shell command. Prose-only, placeholder, or unresolvable commands are SEV-1.

dimension.name: dependency-correctness
dimension.description: Verify declared depends_on edges match the goal graph: no dangling references, no missing edges that would cause execution-order violations, no cycles. Run spec-validate and treat each dep_warning as a SEV-2 candidate: a consumer goal references a file-path stem produced by another goal without a transitive depends_on path.

dimension.name: runtime-state-gating
dimension.description: Goals requiring runtime state (database, services, auth tokens) must depend on the goal that provisions that state. A goal that runs DB queries without depending on fixtures/migrations is SEV-1.

dimension.name: host-container-split
dimension.description: Validate commands must target the correct execution context (host vs container). A PHP command routed to a Node container or vice versa is SEV-2.

dimension.name: objective-acceptance
dimension.description: Acceptance criteria must be objectively decidable. Tautologies, subjective language ("appropriate", "sufficient", "properly"), and criteria that restate the description without adding verifiable detail are SEV-2.

dimension.name: spec-discovery-consistency
dimension.description: Goal specs must be consistent with discovery outputs (docs/architecture/*). A goal referencing entities, endpoints, or bounded contexts not present in discovery is SEV-2. A goal missing entities documented in discovery is SEV-3.

dimension.name: environment-prerequisites
dimension.description: Goals referencing environment variables, services, or credentials must document or depend on their provisioning. Undeclared environment dependencies are SEV-2.

dimension.name: scope-sanity
dimension.description: Goal scope must be well-bounded. Overlapping scopes between concurrent goals (at MaxGoals>1) are SEV-3. Scope arrays with paths outside the project or referencing non-existent directories are SEV-3.

step.title: Score calculation
step.action: Start at 100 points.
For each finding, deduct by severity: SEV-1 = −25, SEV-2 = −15, SEV-3 = −8, SEV-4 = −3.
Floor at 0 (score cannot go negative).
List every finding in a structured table: severity | dimension | goal ID | file:line | description.
step.rule: The score reflects plan QUALITY, not coverage — a perfect plan scores 100.

step.title: Re-verify RESOLVED claims
step.action: If prior audit files exist (docs/architecture/plan-audit-*.md), extract every finding marked RESOLVED in subsequent passes.
For each RESOLVED claim, re-verify it against current on-disk state. If the issue persists, re-open it at its original severity.
step.rule: Never trust a prior pass's resolved-issue ledger without re-verification. A RESOLVED claim is a HYPOTHESIS until re-confirmed against current artifacts.

step.title: Corruption re-sweep
step.action: When a defect is found in one goal (e.g. a missing depends_on edge, a prose validate command, a tautological acceptance criterion), grep the entire .tmux-cli/ tree for the same pattern in other goals.
Record any additional instances as separate findings with the same severity.
step.rule: A systemic defect found in one goal is likely present in others — always sweep.

step.title: Verdict
step.action: Determine the verdict based on the final score and open findings:

substep.title: PASS — write approval
substep.condition: score >= 90 AND zero open SEV-1 AND zero open SEV-2
substep.action: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist.

substep.title: FAIL — write audit findings
substep.condition: score < 90 OR any open SEV-1 OR any open SEV-2
substep.action: Create docs/architecture/ directory if it does not exist.

step.title: Stop condition
step.action: This is a single-pass auditor. After writing the verdict file, print a summary:
If APPROVED: "Plan approved (score {score}/100, pass {PASS_N}). docs/architecture/plan-approval.md written."
If FAILED and PASS_N < 3: "Plan audit failed (score {score}/100, pass {PASS_N}). Fix the {N} findings in docs/architecture/plan-audit-{PASS_N}.md and re-run /tmux:plan-audit."
If FAILED and PASS_N >= 3: "Plan audit failed after 3 passes (score {score}/100). Escalating to human review. Open findings listed in docs/architecture/plan-audit-{PASS_N}.md."
step.rule: The caller drives the re-invocation loop (up to 3 passes). This step only prints guidance.

execution-rules.rule: Read-only: only write docs/architecture/plan-approval.md or docs/architecture/plan-audit-N.md
Role separation: auditor never edits goals, specs, code, or architecture docs (except its own output files)
No interactivity: all decisions autonomous, no AskUserQuestion

Exactly 8 dimensions: validate-executability, dependency-correctness, runtime-state-gating, host-container-split, objective-acceptance, spec-discovery-consistency, environment-prerequisites, scope-sanity
Scoring: 100 baseline; SEV-1 = −25, SEV-2 = −15, SEV-3 = −8, SEV-4 = −3; floor at 0
Approval threshold: score >= 90 AND zero open SEV-1/SEV-2
Single pass per invocation — 3-pass loop is caller-driven
Re-verify every RESOLVED claim from prior passes against current on-disk state
Corruption re-sweep: when a defect pattern is found in one goal, grep the tree for the same pattern

llm.critical-rules: * Auditor is READ-ONLY — only writes plan-approval.md or plan-audit-N.md * Role separation — never edit what you audit (goals, specs, code, architecture docs) * Exactly 8 dimensions — no inventions, no omissions * Scoring: 100 start, SEV-1 = −25, SEV-2 = −15, SEV-3 = −8, SEV-4 = −3 * Approval: score >= 90 AND zero open SEV-1/SEV-2 * Re-verify every RESOLVED claim — never trust a prior pass's ledger without re-confirmation * Corruption re-sweep — a defect in one goal means grep the tree for the same pattern * Single pass per invocation — the 3-pass stop is caller-driven * reach 90% by fixing the plan, never by weakening validate/acceptance to make red turn green * No interactivity — no AskUserQuestion, all decisions autonomous * Dependency-correctness dimension: run spec-validate and treat each dep_warning as a SEV-2 candidate
